title: Moving On

jokes.text: Andy's elaborate defense of his lies about being in the office while sailing, claiming Oscar says hi 'all the time' in multiple languages
jokes.setup: David confronts Andy about lying about being in the office
jokes.punchline: Andy's ridiculous logic that Oscar greeting people proves he wasn't lying
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy showing Andy's delusional logic and ability to twist any situation in his favor

jokes.text: Andy lamenting missing his beard like 'a security blanket' after his breakup
jokes.setup: Andy is explaining how tough things have been
jokes.punchline: Treating facial hair as emotional support
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's childish attachment to things and tendency to overshare

jokes.text: Andy's butchered Bon Jovi reference: 'That's a poem by J.B. Jovi'
jokes.setup: Andy is trying to be profound and grateful
jokes.punchline: Gets the famous musician's name completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - trying to be cultured but revealing his ignorance

jokes.text: Andy's confessional: 'Vanilla Ice. He was a band.'
jokes.setup: Following the ice/Vanilla Ice pun
jokes.punchline: Andy incorrectly thinks Vanilla Ice was a band, not a solo rapper
jokes.explanation: Efficient character comedy showing Andy's cultural confusion in just four words

jokes.text: Andy breaks down crying mid-greeting to camera
jokes.setup: Andy trying to put on a brave face for the cameras
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks down sobbing
jokes.explanation: Strong visual comedy moment showing Andy's complete lack of composure

jokes.text: Andy's talking head becomes increasingly emotional with multiple takes
jokes.setup: Andy trying to do a professional talking head about the breakup
jokes.punchline: Each take gets more emotional until he's wailing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating structure showing Andy's complete inability to maintain professionalism

jokes.text: Dwight: 'He sounds like a wounded animal. Should have put him out of his misery and just fired him.'
jokes.setup: Andy's emotional breakdown echoing through the office
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact suggestion to 'put him out of his misery'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - treating human emotion like farm animal management with clinical detachment

jokes.text: Dwight: 'I can't be around sad people. It makes me sad. I'm the same way with horny people.'
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his discomfort with Andy's sadness
jokes.punchline: The unexpected parallel to being around horny people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic connecting two completely unrelated emotional states with his typical matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Erin: 'I mean, I saw Pete's butt. It's sick.'
jokes.setup: Erin explaining the appeal of their secret relationship
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about Pete's anatomy with slang
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's tendency to overshare and use awkward slang

jokes.text: Pam: 'It's with a real estate company, which is a great fit for me, because I live in a house and I know what a bathroom is.'
jokes.setup: Pam explaining why she's qualified for a real estate job
jokes.punchline: Her 'qualifications' are just basic human experiences
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that plays on interview anxiety and imposter syndrome

jokes.text: Pam: 'my resume can fit on a post-it note'
jokes.setup: Pam being nervous about the interview
jokes.punchline: Vivid image of how minimal her work experience is
jokes.explanation: Efficient visual metaphor that everyone can relate to regarding job hunting anxiety

jokes.text: Dwight's dramatic entrance interrupting Angela with 'I need you'
jokes.setup: Angela taking a quiet break
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sudden dramatic appearance scaring her
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy moment with Dwight's typical dramatic flair

jokes.text: Dwight's aunt Shirley description: 'her hair, her clothes, it's all falling off in great, big clumps'
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his aunt's condition
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely vivid description of her deterioration
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight dark humor treating family tragedy with matter-of-fact description

jokes.text: Dwight: 'We had a nurse, but she quit, because she was "poisoned" by aunt Shirley.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why they need Angela's help
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of the nurse being poisoned
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - starts with needing help, casually drops that someone was poisoned

jokes.text: Dwight: 'Or lemonade and strychnine, which is actually what it was.'
jokes.setup: Angela asking what he means by poisoned
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact confirmation it was actual poison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - treating attempted murder as a minor detail worth clarifying

jokes.text: Dwight's description of aunt Shirley's 'prehensile wing' body part
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to convince Angela to help bathe his aunt
jokes.punchline: Grotesque anatomical description using animal terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly grotesque Dwight description that's both specific and incomprehensible

jokes.text: Clark: 'Not on a three-month boat trip. Oh, burn.'
jokes.setup: Andy asking where Angela is going
jokes.punchline: Clark referencing Andy's boat absence as a comeback
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but Clark awkwardly announcing 'burn' makes it less effective

jokes.text: Andy and Jim's passive-aggressive sweater exchange
jokes.setup: Andy being petty about work messages
jokes.punchline: The sweater becoming a symbol of their relationship deterioration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how breakups make people petty about meaningless objects

jokes.text: Andy coining nicknames: 'Nard Dog, Plop, and Clarker Posey, A.K.A. Clarkwork Orange'
jokes.setup: Andy addressing the guys for advice
jokes.punchline: Elaborate nickname combining Clark with 'A Clockwork Orange'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy overthinking nicknames, though the Clockwork Orange reference shows some wit

jokes.text: Clark's sad bachelor confession: 'Last night I ordered a pizza by myself, and I ate it over the sink like a rat.'
jokes.setup: Trying to console Andy about being single
jokes.punchline: Vivid image of pathetic single behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor with a vivid, relatable image that perfectly captures sad bachelor life

jokes.text: Aunt Shirley: 'Who's this little kitchen witch? She's so tiny, like a little kitchen witch.'
jokes.setup: Meeting Angela for the first time
jokes.punchline: Immediately fixating on the 'kitchen witch' comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction showing Aunt Shirley's blunt, repetitive speaking style

jokes.text: Aunt Shirley: 'How would you like a mean, cold slap?' followed by actually slapping Angela
jokes.setup: Angela offering a nice warm bath
jokes.punchline: Immediate threat followed by actual violence
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation from threat to action, good physical comedy timing

jokes.text: Andy gathering the men: 'Darryl, Clark, Kevin, Toby, plop, take a knee.'
jokes.setup: Andy needs to discuss his relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a football huddle with formal roster call
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his tendency to over-dramatize and create hierarchies

jokes.text: Pete's realization: 'It just occurred to me that Andy has been calling me plop for so long he forgot my real name. Which is Pete.'
jokes.setup: Andy asking if anyone knows a Pete while looking right at him
jokes.punchline: Pete realizing Andy doesn't connect him to his own nickname
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy moment that reveals the absurdity of The Office's nickname culture while advancing the plot

jokes.text: Jim's bluetooth headset: 'I know where you're going with this, and this is who I am now. I'm a douche.'
jokes.setup: Pam noticing Jim's bluetooth earpiece
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately acknowledging he's become what he once mocked
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development joke showing Jim's self-awareness about his transformation while in Philadelphia

jokes.text: Andy's chlamydia lie: 'I just got off the phone with my doctor, and turns out I contracted ch-lamydia from Erin. And it's incurable.'
jokes.setup: Andy confronting Pete about dating Erin
jokes.punchline: Outrageous lie designed to make Pete uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe moment showing Andy's desperation and poor judgment in confrontation

jokes.text: Andy's improvised firing song: '♪ So you had a bad day / ♪ You're a big idiot, get out of my office ♪'
jokes.setup: Andy firing Pete in anger
jokes.punchline: Turning it into a musical number with made-up lyrics
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy absurdity - channeling his anger through impromptu musical theater with terrible lyrics

jokes.text: Andy reading relationship contract: 'Every phrase is like a dagger in my crotch.'
jokes.setup: Andy forced to look at Pete and Erin's relationship paperwork
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and crude description of emotional pain
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment combining his tendency to over-dramatize with crude humor

jokes.text: Mark's Django reference: 'I'm the horrible boss around here, but please don't hire Jamie Foxx to kill me.'
jokes.setup: Mark introducing himself as the boss
jokes.punchline: Awkward movie reference about killing bosses
jokes.explanation: Weak joke relying on pop culture reference without adding anything clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Mark's uncomfortable racism comment: 'I don't agree with the use of the n-word in that movie. It's too soon.'
jokes.setup: Following up the Django reference
jokes.punchline: Completely tone-deaf comment about historical context
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor showing Mark's complete lack of social awareness, though the topic makes it uncomfortable

jokes.text: Mark's forced office enthusiasm: 'They call me Marky Mark around here, because here at Simon realty we are one funky bunch.'
jokes.setup: Mark trying to show office culture
jokes.punchline: Dated reference and forced enthusiasm falling flat
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing a boss trying too hard to be cool with outdated references

jokes.text: Pam's realization: 'Oh, my God. He's Michael Scott.'
jokes.setup: Watching Mark's terrible boss behavior
jokes.punchline: Recognizing the familiar pattern of inappropriate management
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that crystallizes the character type while showing Pam's experience and growth

jokes.text: Angela taking charge of Aunt Shirley care: 'I'm gonna give your aunt a proper bath and a haircut like a lady.'
jokes.setup: Dwight and his aunt arguing about hosing
jokes.punchline: Angela asserting maternal authority over the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's dignity and competence in contrast to Dwight's crude approach

jokes.text: Nellie's Scranton Strangler advice: 'Do something about it. Get it out of your system, whatever it takes.'
jokes.setup: Toby obsessing over the Strangler case again
jokes.punchline: Nellie essentially telling him to visit a dangerous criminal
jokes.explanation: Good callback that advances the running gag while showing Nellie's direct approach to problems

jokes.text: Mark's failed 'chillax' command and Roger's defiant response
jokes.setup: Mark demonstrating his authority with trendy slang
jokes.punchline: Complete failure as Roger openly ignores him
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how ineffective Mark's leadership really is

jokes.text: Mark's accent attempts: '[Brooklyn accent] cawfee talk' and 'Like buttah'
jokes.setup: Mark trying to be relatable and funny
jokes.punchline: Terrible accent work referencing SNL sketches
jokes.explanation: Weak humor relying on old SNL references without any fresh spin or good execution

jokes.text: Andy's Juliet comparison: 'I feel like the guy Juliet dated before Romeo. Probably her boss.'
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his hurt feelings
jokes.punchline: Creating elaborate workplace backstory for Shakespeare characters
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Andy logic that makes Shakespeare about office relationships while revealing his self-pity

jokes.text: Kevin's booger observation and Andy's response: 'My whole life is a booger bubble.'
jokes.setup: Kevin pointing out Andy's snot bubble
jokes.punchline: Andy turning it into existential metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great combination of physical comedy and Andy's tendency to over-dramatize everything into philosophical statements

jokes.text: Mark reading Pam's upside-down resume and his gibberish reaction
jokes.setup: Mark trying to read Pam's resume
jokes.punchline: Speaking nonsense because he's reading it upside down
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy that shows Mark's incompetence but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Mark's existential crisis: 'I mean, they worship me, you know, but do they like me?'
jokes.setup: Mark trying to compliment Pam's loyalty
jokes.punchline: Revealing his deep insecurity and delusion about being worshipped
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that mirrors Michael Scott's insecurities while showing Mark's particular brand of delusion

jokes.text: Mark's Bob Dylan impression during job interview
jokes.setup: Mark asking if Pam has kids and does art
jokes.punchline: Turning her background into a terrible Dylan song
jokes.explanation: Extended cringe moment that goes on too long but effectively shows Mark's complete inappropriateness

jokes.text: Andy's crude translation: 'the ol' one-two punch to my scrotum pole, translation--penis, translation--my manhood'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to have a mature conversation with Pete
jokes.punchline: Multiple increasingly crude euphemisms with unnecessary explanations
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's immaturity and discomfort with direct communication, though the multiple translations are overkill

jokes.text: Mark's pregnancy policy: 'The last three girls here all got pregnant... It's a different chair.'
jokes.setup: Mark explaining the receptionist position
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the chair itself causes pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Mark's complete inability to handle workplace issues appropriately

jokes.text: Mark's receptionist explanation: 'Like a receptionist, but we call you the office manager, because it's less demeaning.'
jokes.setup: Pam questioning if this is just a receptionist job
jokes.punchline: Mark admitting it's just a title change to mask the reality
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about corporate title inflation that resonates with Pam's experience

jokes.text: Pam's final decision: 'I spent ten years as a receptionist to Michael Scott. And I have kids now. And I just-- I can't.'
jokes.setup: Pam realizing this job would be more of the same
jokes.punchline: Simple statement of growth and self-respect
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Pam's growth and self-awareness, perfectly economical and earned

jokes.text: Toby's Bogart impression: '[As Bogart] I think this is the start of my first friendship.'
jokes.setup: Toby psyching himself up to meet the Strangler
jokes.punchline: Misquoting Casablanca while planning to befriend a murderer
jokes.explanation: Good Toby moment combining his pop culture references with his dangerous naivety about the Strangler

jokes.text: Gabe's weight description: 'Feel how fat my buttocks are... It's like a warm pumpkin.'
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to reconnect with his ex
jokes.punchline: Grotesque comparison of his body to produce
jokes.explanation: Peak Gabe awkwardness with a memorably weird metaphor that perfectly captures his bizarre approach to intimacy

jokes.text: Toby's strangling aftermath and Nellie's commentary about 'the proud neck of justice'
jokes.setup: Toby returning from visiting the Strangler
jokes.punchline: Nellie creating heroic mythology around Toby getting strangled
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Strangler storyline with Nellie's grandiose language contrasting with the mundane reality

jokes.text: Aunt Shirley's scarecrow wisdom: 'That's what Mose said about his lady scarecrow, and look what he did to that poor thing.'
jokes.setup: Angela claiming she and Dwight are just friends
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to Mose's relationship with farm equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd line that expands The Office universe's weirdness while staying true to the Schrute farm mythology

jokes.text: Jim's consolation champagne: 'It's from the part of France that immediately gave up to the Nazis.'
jokes.setup: Jim trying to make Pam feel better about not getting the job
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about cheap champagne using WWII reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim line combining historical knowledge with self-deprecating humor about consolation gifts - very quotable and clever

jokes.text: Dwight's life proposal: 'The 80 or 90 years that I have left of this life... I want to spend with you.'
jokes.setup: Dwight making romantic declaration to Angela
jokes.punchline: Typical Dwight precision about his remaining lifespan in a romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - even his romantic declarations include mathematical precision and assumptions about longevity

jokes.text: Gabe's button-down confession: 'I still wear Erin's button-downs around the condo sometimes, so it feels to me like we're still in a relationship'
jokes.setup: Andy asking why the day was weird
jokes.punchline: Gabe revealing disturbing coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Peak Gabe creepiness that perfectly captures his inability to process rejection in a healthy way

jokes.text: Gabe's love confession story: 'She'd plug her ears and scream her heart out.'
jokes.setup: Andy asking if Erin ever said she loved Gabe
jokes.punchline: Gabe thinking Erin's horror was 'adorable'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Gabe's complete inability to read social cues - he found terror 'adorable'

jokes.text: Gabe's KKK music question: 'So you like songs of hate written by the white knights of ku klux klan?'
jokes.setup: Pete saying he likes all kinds of music
jokes.punchline: Gabe taking it to an extreme and disturbing logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Shows Gabe's confrontational nature and tendency toward extreme arguments, though the subject matter is uncomfortable

jokes.text: Gabe's smooth porpoise line: 'I am as smooth as a porpoise for you.'
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to win Alice back by describing his grooming
jokes.punchline: Bizarre marine mammal comparison for romantic appeal
jokes.explanation: Peak Gabe absurdity - a completely original and bizarre metaphor that perfectly captures his weird approach to romance

jokes.text: Jim's Odd Life of Timothy Green poster detail and Pam's reaction
jokes.setup: Pam describing the strange interview
jokes.punchline: The boss crying at an obscure movie poster 20 times a day
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that adds to Mark's weird boss persona, though the specific movie reference is quite niche

jokes.text: Clark's workout philosophy: 'My trainer said, "everybody fails working out." That's how you win.'
jokes.setup: Clark explaining his ab workout boots
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical gym wisdom that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fitness culture's meaningless motivational sayings, fits Clark's try-hard personality

jokes.text: Clark stuck upside-down asking for help while everyone ignores him
jokes.setup: Clark's workout equipment trapping him
jokes.punchline: Extended helplessness while office continues normally
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy showing the office's indifference to Clark and consequences of his fitness obsession

tmdb_overview: Pam interviews for a job in Philadelphia, but her potential manager reminds her of her former boss, Michael Scott. Dwight acquires the help of Angela in order to give his elderly Aunt Shirley a bath. Meanwhile, Andy attempts to make Pete and Erin feel uncomfortable.
